completed_tasks.name: Add D(h) correction to combine path
completed_tasks.notes: posterior_combination.py:combine_posteriors() now calls precompute_completion_denominator() and subtracts n_events*log(D(h)) before combining. Implemented. But see 'findings' — this fix is insufficient.

completed_tasks.name: Cluster-side covariance migration
completed_tasks.notes: Ran on cluster: 'uv run python scripts/migrate_crb_to_ecliptic.py /pfs/work9/workspace/scratch/st_ac147838-emri/run_20260401_seed200/simulations/'. Result: 3 CSVs fully migrated to ecliptic_BarycentricTrue_J2000, 1 cov-rotation-only. Backups in *.bak_equatorial.

completed_tasks.name: Re-run combine with both fixes (job 4148717)
completed_tasks.notes: SLURM job 4148717 COMPLETED (3m08s). MAP=0.86 STILL BIASED. D(h) fix made no meaningful difference. Root cause is not D(h). See 'findings'.

findings.summary: MAP=0.86 persists after both the D(h) fix and covariance migration. Root cause is L_cat exploding at high h, not missing D(h).
findings.root_cause: L_cat (galaxy catalog match term) is 1000-36000x larger at h=0.86 than h=0.73 for ~121 of 312 events. At higher h, the implied luminosity distance is smaller → galaxy search window moves to lower redshift → more GLADE galaxies match (GLADE is more complete at lower z) → L_cat inflated. D(h) should correct for this, but it is also computed incorrectly.
findings.dh_direction_bug: D(h) DECREASES from 4.14e6 at h=0.60 to 3.46e6 at h=0.86. It should INCREASE (more detectable galaxies at closer distances). Suspected cause: P_det grid zero-fills below d_L_min (sources closer than the injection grid minimum get p_det=0 instead of p_det~1). This means the completeness denominator misses the contribution from very close/loud sources at high h, causing D(h) to decrease artificially.
findings.magnitude: D(h) varies only ~20% across the full h range. L_cat varies up to 36000x. Even a correct D(h) cannot compensate unless it also increases by ~36000x (which would require fixing the P_det zero-fill issue).

event_breakdown.note: The few events strongly favoring h=0.86 dominate completely over the many events weakly favoring h=0.73.

findings.note_on_phase43_map073: The MAP=0.730 baseline (baseline.json, n_events=5, symmetric log-posteriors) was from a LOCAL SYNTHETIC test during Phase 43, NOT from the 412-event production data. The production cluster run has always given MAP=0.86.

options_for_next_session.name: Fix P_det zero-fill: use p_det=1 below d_L_min
options_for_next_session.description: In simulation_detection_probability.py, change interpolated_zero_fill to use p_det=1.0 (or linearly extrapolate) for d_L < d_L_min. This is physically correct (sources closer than the injection grid minimum are always louder → always detectable). Also affects D(h) numerically, should make D(h) increase with h. Requires physics-change protocol. Medium-sized code change.
options_for_next_session.expected_outcome: D(h) increases with h, partially correcting the L_cat bias. MAP may shift toward h=0.73 but magnitude uncertain.
options_for_next_session.risk: p_det=1 extrapolation may be too aggressive if the injection grid minimum is not well-defined. May need to check injection data coverage.

options_for_next_session.name: Restrict galaxy catalog to a redshift floor
options_for_next_session.description: Add z_min cutoff to the GLADE search (e.g. z > 0.01) to avoid contamination from very nearby galaxies. This prevents the 'nearby galaxy explosion' of L_cat at high h.
options_for_next_session.expected_outcome: Removes the strongest high-delta events. May shift MAP toward 0.73.
options_for_next_session.risk: May discard genuine host galaxies of nearby events. Needs justification from catalog completeness data.

options_for_next_session.name: Debug the high-delta events individually
options_for_next_session.description: Pick event 113 (f_i=0.561, L_cat jumps 36000x) and trace exactly which galaxies are matched at h=0.73 vs h=0.86. Check if it is a single massive galaxy causing the jump, and whether that galaxy is the true host or an accidental match.
options_for_next_session.expected_outcome: Precise diagnosis of the catalog contamination mechanism.
options_for_next_session.risk: Low — diagnostic only. But may not immediately suggest a fix.

options_for_next_session.name: Accept MAP=0.86 and document as incompleteness bias
options_for_next_session.description: Do not change the pipeline. Characterize the bias as arising from GLADE incompleteness correction failure at the current implementation level. Report in the paper with an estimated bias correction.
options_for_next_session.expected_outcome: No code changes. Paper acknowledges known bias. Faster to thesis completion.
options_for_next_session.risk: H0 result would be biased +16% from truth. Reviewers may object.

recommended_next_action: Start with /gpd:debug. The bug is in simulation_detection_probability.py:detection_probability_without_bh_mass_interpolated_zero_fill — the zero-fill is physically wrong for d_L below the injection grid minimum. Option A is the likely correct fix. But first: confirm via Option C (trace event 113 at h=0.73 vs h=0.86) to make sure the hypothesis is correct before changing P_det. Physics-change protocol required for any P_det modification.
